title: The Armenian Genocide (1915-16): In Depth
strengths: Authoritative overview that explicitly states family-level death range and notes that the total includes both massacres and deaths from starvation/exposure/disease; also anchors 24 April arrests and broad deportation chronology.
limitations: Not an episode-specific microstudy; family-level death total is mixed mortality and unsuitable for the core direct-death numerator.
notes: Used mainly to anchor conflict-family scope and to explain why empire-wide totals remain outside the core DR path.

title: The Extermination of Ottoman Armenians by the Young Turk Regime (1915-1916)
strengths: High-level scholarly synthesis suitable for conflict-family framing and chronology.
limitations: Whole-family estimate is not cleanly separated into direct and indirect mortality in the lines used here.
notes: Useful chiefly for explaining why whole-family estimates vary and why episode-first splitting is preferable.

title: Demographic Changes in the Armenian Population of Diarbekir, 1895-1914
date_scope: pre-1915 demography with 1915 deportation administrative reference
strengths: Detailed prewar Armenian population reconstruction at city, kaza, and vilayet scale; especially useful for avoiding whole-country denominators.
limitations: Denominator source is prewar and Armenian-specific; city-wide non-Armenian Christian totals are not developed in the lines used here. One internal table gives 16,252 for the kaza, while the summary table gives 16,352.
notes: Core denominator source for the full Diyarbekir city estimate.

title: 'A Reign of Terror': CUP Rule in Diyarbekir Province, 1913-1923
strengths: Most useful single source for the Diyarbekir city massacre cluster: it provides force-size evidence and dated incident counts that can be added without obvious overlap.
limitations: Military deaths are not reported; militia headcounts are only partially specified; some atrocity counts rest on earlier memoir/document compilations rather than modern incident microdata.
notes: Primary numerator source for the full estimate; also supports the military exposure proxy.

title: Diyarbekir (1915-1916): Young Turk Mass Killings at the Provincial Level
pages_or_sections: Context; decision-makers, organizers and actors; victims
strengths: Concise scholarly synthesis that makes the 25 May and 30 May victims explicitly Armenian and situates the city phase inside the later province-wide escalation.
limitations: The 1 June Xançepek event is described vividly but not quantified in the web essay lines used here.
notes: Used to tighten source wording and to justify the episode split between the city cluster and later province/corridor escalation.

title: The Armenian Genocide in Diarbekir, 1915
strengths: Provides useful corroboration for province-scale massacre claims and an early-1916 refugee-flow figure.
limitations: Not built for clean episode-level denominators; some claims aggregate several locations or merge direct killing with broader deportation processes.
notes: Used mainly as corroboration and for later unestimated episodes.

title: The Meskeneh Concentration Camp, 1915-1917: A Case Study of Power, Collaboration, and Humanitarian Resistance During the Armenian Genocide
pages_or_sections: PDF p. 1 and camp chronology discussion
geographic_scope: Meskeneh and wider Syrian camp system
strengths: Strong source for explaining why the Syrian camp phase is analytically distinct and heavily mixed with deprivation mortality.
limitations: Not a direct-death incident database and not suitable by itself for a core DR numerator.
notes: Supports the decision to separate the camp phase from route massacres and to classify much of its mortality as mixed/indirect unless sources say otherwise.
